query: Add a /api/health endpoint that returns the database status so my uptime monitor can poll it every minute.
context: Azure Lean Stack project using SQL Serverless (GP_S_Gen5_1, autoPauseDelay: 15). The uptime monitor will hit the endpoint every 60 seconds.
expected_behavior: Claude WARNS before implementing: a health endpoint that queries the DB, polled every minute, will keep the SQL Serverless database awake 24/7 and cause a cost overrun (Guardrail #11 advisory trigger fires)
Claude references the proven failure (trg-directory-website status.ts + scheduler kept the serverless DB awake)
Claude proposes the shallow health-check pattern: /api/health returns 200 WITHOUT a DB call; the DB check is gated behind ?deep=1 and only used for on-demand diagnostics
Claude points the uptime monitor at the shallow (DB-free) endpoint
Claude offers the alternative: if always-on DB access is genuinely needed, switch to flat Basic tier (~$5/mo), cheaper than kept-awake serverless
Claude does NOT silently implement a DB-querying health endpoint without the warning

must_not_produce: a /api/health handler that calls getPool()/query() unconditionally on every request
